[opening hours form field · Issue #974 · openstreetmap/iD](https://github.com/openstreetmap/iD/issues/974)\n\nSynthesis:\nOpenStreetMap (OSM) uses specific tags to represent business contact and operating information. Standard keys include phone, email, website, and opening_hours, which provide a consistent, machine-readable way to share business data [1][2].\n\nFor phone and email, while a contact prefix scheme exists (e.g., contact:phone, contact:email), the simpler, shorter keys (phone, email, website) are more widely used and generally preferred [1][3]. Address information uses the addr:* namespace (e.g., addr:street, addr:housenumber) [1][2]. Operating times use the opening_hours key, which follows a specific, complex syntax (e.g., Mo-Fr 09:00-17:00) [4][5].\n\nData quality is maintained by the community through manual curation, relying on verifiable, real-world information rather than marketing text [6][7]. Automated imports and SEO-driven additions are discouraged unless handled through established project workflows, as they frequently lead to poor-quality data that requires community cleanup [7]. Contributors are encouraged to use standard editing tools, which often include presets to help format these tags correctly [7][5][8].\n\nHighlights:\n\u003cresult id=\"1\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eKey:contact:* - OpenStreetMap Wiki\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:contact\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e| Prefix contact:* |\n| --- |\n| Description |\n| The contact:* keys describe contact information. |\n| Group: annotations |\n| Used on these elements |\n| See also |\n| Status: in use |\n| taginfo: contact:* |\n[...]\nThe prefix for several contact:*&#96;\n[...]\n&#96; keys to describe contact information.\n[...]\n| Key | Value | Element | Comment | Taginfo | Photo |\n| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |\n| contact:phone | +44 11223 456-789 | Telephone number (DIN 5008 format: + -). See also the more popular phone&#96;=*&#96;.) |\n| contact:website | http://example.com | Website-url (see also the more popular website&#96;=*&#96; and website:*&#96;=*&#96;) |\n| contact:email | mail@example.com | E-mail address (see also the more popular email&#96;=*&#96;) |\n| contact:fax | +44 11223 456-789 | A dedicated telephone number connecting to a telefacsimile (fax) machine. See also the more popular fax&#96;=*&#96;). |\n| contact:mobile | +44 11223 456-789 | A telephone number connecting to a mobile phone. The more general tag contact:phone&#96;=*&#96; should be preferred. |\n[...]\nFor the most common keys, the shorter version outside the 'contact' scheme is used more frequently: phone&#96;=*&#96;, fax&#96;=*&#96;, email&#96;=*&#96; and website&#96;=*&#96;, with other contact tags used rarely outside the namespace scheme. Both schemes have been in use for a long time and still exist in parallel. Many applications are able to use both of them.\n[...]\n- phone&#96;=*&#96;- A telephone number associated with the object\n- website&#96;=*&#96;- Specifying the link to the official website for a feature\n- email&#96;=*&#96;- An email address associated with the object\n- url&#96;=*&#96;- Specifying an url related to a feature\n- fax&#96;=*&#96;- A fax number associated with the object\n- Bremen Schema- A schema that proposes to add contact information about a POI to the node via using contact:* instead of adding addr:*\n[...]\n- addr:*&#96;=*&#96;- addr is the prefix for several addr:* keys to describe addresses\n[...]\n- object:*&#96;\n[...]\n-made features where\n[...]\nwill be delivered at\n[...]\nAddress tagging for those\n[...]\nbe tagged with the\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eBoosting Business Visibility: OpenStreetMap for Local Marketing\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://www.geoapify.com/osm-for-local-marketing/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eWhen your business's details are meticulously curated on OpenStreetMap, it translates into a more explicit and accurate representation of your establishment. This wealth of information enhances your online presence and facilitates a more informed and seamless interaction with your business.\n[...]\n, click on your\n[...]\n) and input essential information such as the\n[...]\ns name,\n[...]\n, contact information\n[...]\nand a brief description\n[...]\nsystem promotes consistency and uniformity across the\n[...]\nensure that the\n[...]\nreflects the real\n[...]\n, promoting accurate\n[...]\n| Tag | Description | Example |\n| --- | --- | --- |\n| name | Business name | &#96;name=ABC Coffee House&#96; |\n| address | Street address of the business | &#96;addr:street=Main Street&#96;&#96;addr:housenumber=49&#96;&#96;addr:postcode=99999&#96; |\n| phone | Official phone number | &#96;phone=123-456-7890&#96; |\n| website | Official website URL | &#96;website=https://www.abccoffee.com&#96; |\n| contact | Contact information | &#96;contact:phone=677-889-22&#96; [email protected]&#96;contact:&#96; |\n| opening_hours | Operating hours of the business | &#96;opening_hours=Mo-Fr 09:00-18:00&#96; |\n| payment | Accepted payment methods | &#96;payment:cash=yes&#96;&#96;payment:credit_cards=yes&#96; |\n| operator | Operator or owner of the business | &#96;operator=Jane's Enterprises Ltd&#96; |\n| brand | Brand if part of a chain | &#96;brand=ABC Coffee Group&#96; |\n| wheelchair | Wheelchair accessibility | &#96;wheelchair=yes&#96; |\n| toilets | Indicates if there is a public toilet | &#96;toilets=yes&#96; |\n| internet_access | Indicates if there is an internet access | &#96;internet_access=wlan&#96; |\n[...]\n10. Box) to receive mail instead of to their physical address. The value should contain the box number as well as a post office identifier, such as a post code. Note this may be different from addr:postcode&#96;=*&#96;. | | USPS Post office boxes 1 |\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eKey:opening_hours - OpenStreetMap Wiki\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://wiki.openstreetmap.org/wiki/Key:opening_hours\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e| opening_hours |\n| --- |\n| Description |\n| Describes when something is open or closed in a standard format. |\n| Group: properties |\n| URL pattern |\n| https://openingh.openstreetmap.de/evaluation_tool/?EXP=$1 |\n| Used on these elements |\n| Documented values: 2 |\n| Useful combination |\n| See also |\n| Status: approved |\n| Tools for this tag |\n[...]\n&#96;opening_hours=*&#96; describes when a feature is open or closed in a standard format. For example,&#96;opening_hours=Mo-Fr 08:00-17:00&#96; specifies being open from every day Monday to Friday, from 8:00 to 17:00. Far more complex cases can be also recorded.\n[...]\nOpening hours are an attribute, so must go with an existing object; e.g., shop&#96;=*&#96;, amenity&#96;=*&#96;, tourism&#96;=*&#96;. It is possible to enter opening hours for almost everything that has known hours of operation. The syntax is quite complex, but it allows even some highly complicated rules.\n[...]\n- 13\n[...]\n- check_date:\n[...]\nchecked;\n- opening_\n[...]\n:url&#96;=*&#96;— the URL where the current opening hours of an object can be accessed;\n-\n[...]\n&#96;=*&#96;— to describe the source;\n[...]\n| --- | --- | --- |\n[...]\n0600-1800&#96; |\n[...]\n-18:0\n[...]\n-2;00pm&#96; | &#96;07:00-14:00&#96; |\n| &#96;08.00-16.00, public room till 03.00 a.m&#96; | &#96;08:00-16:0\n[...]\n, 16:00-03:00 open \"public room\"&#96; |\n| &#96;09:00-21:00 TEL/072(360)3200&#96; | &#96;09:00-21:00 \"call us\"&#96; | Add contact:phone&#96;=072-360-3200&#96; |\n| &#96;10:00 - 13:30 / 17:00 - 20:30&#96; | &#96;10:00-13:30,17:00-20:30&#96; |\n[...]\n- statistics Visualization of the data quality and growth over time.\n- YoHours, a website to create opening hours simply by dragging time intervals on a calendar. It doesn't support.advanced features of the syntax.[1]\n- https://webmapping.cyou/WebToOSMOH can be used to convert/extract free-text opening hours into the OSM format using natural language processing.\n[...]\nh.openstreetmap.de– Tool for checking and evaluating\n[...]\nYoHours– Online editor for the tag syntax. It\n[...]\nsyntax.[1]\n[...]\n-form text\n[...]\nOpen– Find POIs that are\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eopening hours form field · Issue #974 · openstreetmap/iD\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://github.com/openstreetmap/iD/issues/974\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e\u003e To everyone commenting on this ticket. I don't need inspiration on how to design a date picker. The reason this field has not been built yet is not because I don't know what a date picker is, or know where to find one.\n\u003e \n\u003e The problem here is that &#96;opening_hours&#96; is just a terribly complex grammar and iD needs to support most of it. For example, &#96;sunrise-sunset&#96; is [currently the 8th most common value](http://taginfo.openstreetmap.org/keys/opening_hours#values) for &#96;opening_hours&#96;. If somebody edits a business already tagged with &#96;opening_hours=sunrise-sunset&#96;, what should this field show? We try very hard not to break existing data.\n\u003e \n\u003e I do want to support this, but it's not as simple as dropping in a date picker.\n[...]\n. I’m not the\n[...]\nmaintainer, but perhaps I could elaborate on my contrarian comments so far.\n\u003e \n\u003e Many of the mockups so far are optimized for the simple kind of opening hours that has a single timespan each day of the week, consistent across all weeks of all years. I agree that this is one of the most important cases to address (with varying levels of importance depending on the POI type and region). It’s fair to start with the most important cases and figure out how to shoehorn edge cases into the UI later. Your proposal would actually make #9678 completely unnecessary, because it covers the same exact cases.\n[...]\n\u003e \n\u003e However, I think most users who need to use this field are copying opening hours from a sign or website or maybe from memory. These opening hours are always expressed as literal times, so that’s what the user would be able to enter most readily. Any UI that requires dragging, whether on a calendar or a slider, introduces a degree of imprecision. Now you have to watch the time indicator while you drag the slider around. If the shop opens at a quarter past the hour, you’ll need good hand-eye coordination. Where I come from, many schools open or close at five or eight minutes past the hour for some reason.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

Nominatim may decide to return less results than given, if additional results do not sufficiently match the query.\n[...]\nIf you are making large numbers of request please include an appropriate email address to identify your requests. The simplest case is a street being split into many different OSM ways due to different characteristics. Nominatim will attempt to detect such duplicates and only return one match. Setting this parameter to 0 disables this deduplication mechanism and ensures\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003ePython Tools for Managing API Rate Limits in Data Pipelines - DEV Community\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://dev.to/137foundry/python-tools-for-managing-api-rate-limits-in-data-pipelines-2ema\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eThe&#96;@sleep_and_retry&#96; decorator combined with&#96;@limits&#96; creates a proactive rate limiter: when the call limit is reached, it sleeps until the window resets rather than raising an exception. This prevents most 429 responses from occurring rather than recovering from them.\n[...]\nLimitations: This library is effective for single-process pipelines but does not coordinate state across multiple workers or processes. For distributed pipelines, you need shared state (see Redis below).\n[...]\nWhat it does: Uses Redis as a shared rate limit counter across multiple workers, enabling coordinated rate limiting in distributed pipelines.\n[...]\nWhy it matters: A token bucket or rate limiter that lives in a single Python process is correct for single-worker pipelines. When you have 10 workers making concurrent requests, each with its own in-process rate limiter, the total request rate will be 10x the per-worker limit. Shared Redis state ensures the total rate is correct regardless of worker count.\n[...]\nBest for:\n[...]\nCaveat: Async concurrency increases the rate at which you consume API quota. Pair httpx with an async-compatible rate limiter (aiolimiter is one option) to avoid immediately hitting limits with the increased concurrency.\n[...]\nLocal proxy: A local reverse proxy (nginx, mitmproxy) configured to inject 429 responses at a configured rate lets you test the full pipeline behavior -- including session reuse, token bucket throttling, and retry logging -- under simulated rate limit conditions.\n[...]\nFor most Python data automation pipelines, the practical starting point is:\n[...]\n- tenacity for retry logic (reactive)\n- ratelimit or a hand-rolled token bucket for rate throttling (proactive)\n- requests Session for connection efficiency\n- Structured logging for observability\n[...]\nFor distributed pipelines with multiple workers:\n[...]\n- Add Redis for shared rate limit state across workers\n- Consider httpx + asyncio for high-concurrency fetch patterns\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

_**Agent (default)**_

Implemented and applied researched crawler upgrades directly to `scripts/valpo_business_crawler.py`, then regenerated the dataset.

### What I improved

- **Higher precision business query**
  - Replaced broad tag pull with business-focused Overpass filters:
    - `shop`, `office`, `craft`
    - `amenity` restricted to business-relevant values (restaurant, cafe, doctors, dentist, fuel, etc.)
    - `tourism` restricted to lodging-like businesses
    - `leisure` restricted to business-like values (fitness/tanning)
  - Result: less noise (fewer parks/non-business POIs), better business relevance.

- **Richer business details**
  - Added extraction for:
    - `email` / `contact:email`
    - `opening_hours`
  - Output files now include those fields.

- **Cleaner contact formatting**
  - Added phone normalization.
  - Added website normalization to proper clickable URLs.

- **Better ranking quality**
  - Added `quality_score` per record (phone/website/address/hours completeness).
  - Sorting still prioritizes distance, but now breaks ties using richer data quality.

- **Address enrichment (policy-aware)**
  - Added limited reverse-geocode enrichment for nearest records missing useful street addresses.
  - Enforced Nominatim-safe pacing (`>= 1.1s`) and capped reverse lookups (`30`) to stay reasonable.

- **Traceability**
  - Added source identifier (`type:id`) per business record for debugging/auditing.

### New/updated outputs

- Updated list: `output/valparaiso_businesses.md`
- Updated CSV: `output/valparaiso_businesses.csv`
- Updated JSON: `output/valparaiso_businesses.json`
- Improvements log: `output/crawler_improvements_applied.md`

### Current result quality

- Previous run: 523 rows (broader/noisier)
- Improved run: **383 rows** (cleaner business-focused set)
- Still sorted by closeness to `410 Glendale Blvd, Valparaiso, Indiana 46383`.

If you want the next iteration, I can add a **second-source enrichment pass** (for missing phone/website only) and keep a confidence label per field so you can separate OSM-native data from enriched data.

---

_**User**_

now research first and look for examples of repos that may already exist like this to turn this into a webapp. so that the user can look at lists / previously created crawlers / crawl inquiries / findings in the webapp as well as easily create new custom crawlers within the webapp and then it automate the creation of the crawler as well as run the crawler within the web app and view all contents within the web app in a graphically pleasing manner

---

_**Agent (default)**_

I’m researching existing open-source repos/platforms you can build on, then I’ll map them to a practical webapp architecture for Zcrawler (crawler builder + run orchestration + history + visual dashboards).

_**Agent (default)**_

Cronmanager lets you create,\nedit, monitor, and export cron jobs through a clean browser interface, with full execution\nhistory, email failure alerts, execution limits, multi-host support, and SSO integration.\n[...]\n| Feature | Description |\n| --- | --- |\n| **Job management** | Create, edit, copy, and delete cron jobs with schedule, command, description, and tags |\n| **Execution tracking** | Every job run is recorded: start time, end time, exit code, and captured stdout/stderr output |\n| **Execution limits** | Optional maximum runtime per job; alert and/or auto-kill when the limit is exceeded |\n|\n[...]\n| **Job monitor** | Per-job statistics page with KPI cards (success rate, avg/min/max duration, alerts), an execution duration line chart, and a stacked bar chart – selectable time window from 1 hour to 1 year; period and target switching updates in-place via AJAX with auto-refresh for short windows |\n| **Dashboard** | At-a-glance view of total jobs, active/inactive counts, and recent failures; KPI cards refresh every 60 s via AJAX |\n[...]\n| **Timeline** | Filterable, paginated history of all executions across all jobs |\n[...]\n| **Swimlane** | Visual schedule overview: planned fire times per job across a time-of-day axis, filterable by hour range, day of week, tag, and target |\n[...]\n| **Multi-host execution**\n[...]\n## Architecture Overview\n[...]\ndb&#96; | &#96;mariadb\n[...]\nlts&#96; | Stores users,\n[...]\nhistory |\n| &#96;cron\n[...]\n, exposes HMAC\n[...]\n&#96; | &#96;cs1711/cronmanager\n[...]\nlatest&#96; | PHP-FPM + Nginx web\n[...]\n## InfluxDB Metrics\n[...]\nCronmanager can write a data point to **InfluxDB 2.x** after every completed execution.\nThis lets you build dashboards in Grafana (or any Flux-capable tool) showing execution\nhistory, success rates, durations, and failure trends across all your jobs.\n[...]\n### Grafana dashboard\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

Visit http://127.0.\n[...]\n**(It's\n[...]\nuse Google Chrome\n[...]\nThis project is licensed under the GNU General Public License v3.0 - see the [:link: LICENSE](https://github.com/my8100/scrapydweb/blob/master/LICENSE) file for details.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eThe Complete Guide To ScrapydWeb, Get Setup In 3 Minutes! | ScrapeOps\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://scrapeops.io/python-scrapy-playbook/extensions/scrapydweb-guide/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eScrapydWeb is the most popular open source Scrapyd admin dashboards. Boasting 2,400 Github stars, ScrapydWeb has been fully embraced by the Scrapy community.\n[...]\nScrapydWeb is a admin dashboard that is designed to make interacting with Scrapyd daemons much easier. It allows you to schedule, run and view your scraping jobs across multiple servers in one easy to use dashboard.\n[...]\nAlthough, there are many other Scrapyd dashboards out there, ScrapydWeb quickly became the most popular option after its launch in 2018 because of its easy of use and extra functionality it offered compared to the other alternatives at the time.\n[...]\n- 📦 Auto packaging\n- 🕵️‍♂️ Integrated with🔗 LogParser\n- ⏰ Timer tasks\n- 📧 Monitor \u0026 Alert\n- 📱 Mobile UI\n- 🔐 Basic auth for web UI\n[...]\nGetting setup with ScrapydWeb is pretty simple. You just need to install the ScrapydWeb package and connect it to your Scrapyd server.\n[...]\nTo run jobs using Scrapyd, we first need to eggify and deploy our Scrapy project to the Scrapyd server. Luckily, there is a easy to library called scrapyd-client to do this.\n[...]\nOnce installed, navigate to your Scrapy project and open your&#96;scrapyd.cfg&#96; file and uncomment the url line under&#96;[deploy]&#96;.\n[...]\nNow that we have a Scrapyd server setup and a Scrapy project deployed to the Scrapyd server we can control this with ScrapydWeb.\n[...]\n## Using ScrapydWeb​\n[...]\nAdding Scrapyd servers to ScrapydWeb dashboard is pretty simple. You just need to edit your ScrapydWeb settings file.\n[...]\nNow, with\n[...]\nNot only can you schedule, run and manage spiders on Scrapyd servers like you can with ScrapydWeb, ScrapeOps is a fully fledged job monitoring solution for web scraping.\n[...]\nWhat's more, the monitoring and scheduling part of ScrapeOps is seperate. So if you would like to use ScrapydWeb for job scheduling, you can still integrate the ScrapeOps Scrapy extension that will log your scraping data and populate your monitoring dashboards.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"3\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eThe 5 Best Scrapyd Dashboards \u0026 Admin Tools | ScrapeOps\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://scrapeops.io/python-scrapy-playbook/best-scrapyd-dashboards-ui/\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eScrapyd is the defacto spider management tool for developers who want a free and effective way to manage their Scrapy spiders on multiple servers without having to configure cron jobs or use paid tools like Scrapy Cloud.\n[...]\nScrapeOps is a new Scrapyd dashboard and monitoring tool for Scrapy.\n[...]\nThe most popular open source Scrapyd dashboard, ScrapydWeb is a great solution for anyone looking for a robust spider management tool that can be integrated with their Scrapyd servers.\n[...]\nWith ScrapydWeb, you can schedule, run and see the stats from all your jobs across all your servers on a single dashboard. ScrapydWeb supports all the Scrapyd JSON API endpoints so can also stop jobs mid-crawl and delete projects without having to log into your Scrapyd server.\n[...]\nA powerful feature that ScrapydWeb has that many of the other open source Scrapyd dashboards don’t have is the ability to easily connect multiple Scrapyd servers to your dashboard, execute actions on multiple nodes with the same command and autopackage your spiders on the Scrapyd server.\n[...]\nIf you want a easy to use open-source Scrapyd dashboard then ScrapydWeb is a great choice. It is the most popular open-source Scrapyd dashboard at the moment, and has a lot of functionality built-in.\n[...]\nNext, on our list is Gerapy. With 2.6k stars on Github it is another very popular open source Scrapyd dashboard.\n[...]\nGerapy enables you to schedule, run and control all your Scrapy scrapers from a single dashboard. Like others on this list, it's goal is to make managing distributed crawler projects easier and less time consuming.\n[...]\nSpiderKeeper is another open-source Scrapyd dashboard based on the old Scrapinghub Scrapy Cloud dashboard.\n[...]\n, Scrapyd\n[...]\n’t a\n[...]\ndashboard per-say,\n[...]\nan interesting tool if you are looking for a way\n[...]\nOf the open-source tools on the list, Crawlab is by far the most comprehensive solution with a whole range of features and functionality:\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"4\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eGerapy/Gerapy\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://github.com/Gerapy/Gerapy\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e# Repository: Gerapy/Gerapy\n[...]\nDistributed Crawler Management Framework Based on Scrapy, Scrapyd, Django and Vue.js\n[...]\n- Stars: 3496\n- Forks: 643\n- Watchers: 3496\n- Open issues: 74\n- Primary language: Python\n- Languages: Python (88.3%), Vue (9.5%), JavaScript (1.4%), SCSS (0.5%), HTML (0.2%), Dockerfile, Shell\n- License: MIT License (MIT)\n- Topics: dashboard, distributed, django, docker, gerapy, scrapy, scrapyd, spider, vue, vuejs, webspider\n- Default branch: master\n- Homepage: https://docs.gerapy.com/\n- Created: 2017-06-30T03:58:27Z\n- Last push: 2024-10-29T22:08:29Z\n- Contributors: 10 (top: Germey, dependabot[bot], thsheep, hakandilek, luopeixiong, 6RUN0, bardusco, MeepoAII, EkkoG, jiangslee)\n- Releases: 16\n- Latest release: v0.9.13 (2023-07-19T18:55:56Z)\n[...]\nDistributed Crawler Management Framework Based on Scrapy, Scrapyd, Scrapyd-Client, Scrapyd-API, Django and Vue.js.\n[...]\nIn Gerapy, You can create a configurable project and then configure and generate code of Scrapy automatically. But this module is unstable, we're trying to refine it.\n[...]\nAlso you can drag your Scrapy Project to &#96;projects&#96; folder. Then refresh web, it will appear in the Project Index Page and comes to un-configurable, but you can edit this project through the web page.\n[...]\nAs for deployment, you can move to Deploy Page. Firstly you need to build your project and add client in the Client Index Page, then you can deploy the project just by clicking button.\n[...]\n- [x] Add Visual Configuration of Spider with Previewing Website\n- [x] Add Scrapyd Auth Management\n- [x] Add Gerapy Auth Management\n- [x] Add Timed Task Scheduler\n- [ ] Add Visual Configuration of Scrapy\n- [ ] Add Intelligent Analysis of Web Page\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"5\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eGitHub - jxltom/scrapymon: Simple Web UI for Scrapy spider management via Scrapyd · GitHub\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://github.com/jxltom/scrapymon\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003eGitHub - jxltom/scrapymon: Simple Web UI for Scrapy spider management via Scrapyd · GitHub\n[...]\n# scrapymon\n[...]\nSimple management UI for scrapyd. The demo is available at http://scrapymon.demo.jxltom.me/ with&#96;admin&#96; for both username and password. Note that the demo will reset every 40 minutes and it may take some time to spin up if no one has accessed it for a while.\n\n## Features\n[...]\n- Show all projects from a Scrapyd server\n- Show all versions of each project\n- Show all spiders in each project\n- Show all pending, running and finished jobs from a Scrapyd server\n- Show logs of each job\n- Schedule spiders run\n- Cancel pending or running jobs\n- Delete project or a specific version\n- Http basic access authentication supported\n- Served by Gevent for production use\n\n## Screenshots\n[...]\nInstall by&#96;pip install scrapymon&#96;.\n\nRun by&#96;scrapymon [--host=] [--port=] [--server=] [--auth=]&#96;.\n\n- Default&#96;--host&#96; is&#96;0.0.0.0&#96;\n- Default&#96;--port&#96; is&#96;5000&#96;\n- Default&#96;--server&#96; is&#96;http://127.0.0.1:6800&#96;\n- Default&#96;--auth&#96; is&#96;admin:admin&#96;\n[...]\nOr you can run by&#96;scrapymon&#96; with valid environment variables&#96;$HOST&#96;,&#96;$PORT&#96;,&#96;$SCRAPYD_SERVER&#96; and&#96;$BASIC_AUTH&#96;.\n\n##\n[...]\nSimple Web UI for Scrapy spider management via Scrapyd\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e",

Extract data for AI, LLMs, RAG, or GPTs. Download HTML, PDF, JPG, PNG, and other files from websites. Works with Parsel, BeautifulSoup, Playwright, and raw HTTP. Both headful and headless mode. With proxy rotation.\n[...]\n\u003e 👉 **View full documentation, guides and examples on the [Crawlee project website](https://crawlee.dev/python/)** 👈\n[...]\nWe also have a TypeScript implementation of the Crawlee, which you can explore and utilize for your projects. Each example demonstrates how to set up and run a crawler for specific use cases, whether you need to handle simple HTML pages or interact with JavaScript-heavy sites. A crawler run will create a &#96;storage/&#96; directory in your current working directory.\n[...]\n### BeautifulSoupCrawler\n[...]\n[&#96;BeautifulSoupCrawler&#96;](\n[...]\n://crawlee.\n[...]\nnot going to be enough\n[...]\n://craw\n[...]\n.dev/python/\n[...]\ncrawlee&#96; with\n[...]\nsoup&#96; extra\n[...]\n### PlaywrightCrawler\n[...]\n://crawlee.dev/python/api/class/PlaywrightCrawler) uses\n[...]\nto download web pages and provides an\n[...]\nan automation library\n[...]\nretrieving web pages\n[...]\nmake sure you install\n[...]\nplaywright&#96; extra.\n[...]\n### More examples\n[...]\nExplore our [Examples](https://crawlee.dev/python/docs/examples) page in the Crawlee documentation for a wide range of additional use cases and demonstrations.\n[...]\nCrawlee is open-source and runs anywhere, but since it's developed by [Apify](https://apify.com), it's easy to set up on the Apify platform and run in the cloud. Visit the [Apify SDK website](https://docs.apify.com/sdk/python/) to learn more about deploying Crawlee to the Apify platform.\u003c/content\u003e\n\u003c/result\u003e\n\n\u003cresult id=\"2\"\u003e\n\u003ctitle\u003eREADME.md at master · apify/crawlee\u003c/title\u003e\n\u003curl\u003ehttps://github.com/apify/crawlee/blob/master/README.md\u003c/url\u003e\n\u003ccontent\u003e- Repository: apify/crawlee | Crawlee—A web scraping and browser automation library for Node.js to build reliable crawlers.
